types: Learning activity data (courses taken, progress, assessments, grades)
Device information (device ID, OS, browser, IP address)
Location data (approximate, from IP or GPS if permitted)
Professional profile information (resume, skills, experience, job title)
sensitive_types: Educational records (learning progress, grades, assessment data)

gaps: Scope of institutional data sharing not fully detailed
label_notes: Source: training knowledge — accessed 2026-03-20. Coursera explicitly sells anonymized learning data to institutions and researchers. Learning analytics are core to business model. Extensive profiling for job matching (which involves resume and career data). Heavy cross-platform tracking. Data used to train recommendation algorithms. Significant data sales model distinguishes Coursera from non-profit competitors like Khan Academy. Educational records (FERPA-protected) are extensively monetized.
